$comment: Complete style map for the Reusable Site Template. Purpose: let an agent author a new, drop-in-replaceable theme. Read meta.how_to_create_a_theme first, then `tokens` (the swappable vocabulary), then `elements` (what each token paints) and `stage` (the animated Home hero, which has theme-specific literals to override). Generated 2026-06-26 against the live CSS.

meta.styling_model: CSS design tokens. No raw color/font literals in markup. Brand-level appearance flows through CSS custom properties (:root) that a theme overrides; only intentionally-decorative art (planets, starfield, nebula) is hardcoded.

files.token_emitter: resources/views/partials/tokens.blade.php  (renders <style>:root{ --token: value }</style> in <head>; merges config('tokens.defaults') with SiteSetting.branding)
files.branding_store: SiteSetting.branding (DB JSON column; the active theme's token overrides)
files.master_layout: resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php (renders partials/cosmos before content; exposes @stack('head') for page-specific stylesheets)
files.shared_backdrop: resources/views/partials/cosmos.blade.php + public/css/cosmos.css — a fixed, full-viewport starfield/nebula/twinkle layer behind EVERY page (z-index:-1). Same cosmos art as the old stage, MINUS the solar system and the mouse parallax / 3D movement (gentle ambient drift only). The .twinkle node is populated by solarsystem.js.
css_load_order: public/css/cosmos.css           (shared fixed starfield backdrop; behind all pages)
files.stage_js: public/js/solarsystem.js (twinkle-star generation into .twinkle + mouse parallax bound to the solar system; self-guards on .stage so the parallax is inert on non-Home pages)

activation.apply_theme: php artisan app:apply-theme {name}   (writes config('themes.{name}') into SiteSetting.branding)
activation.reset: php artisan app:apply-theme default          (clears branding → back to config/tokens.php light defaults)

how_to_create_a_theme: 1. PALETTE/FONTS ONLY (recolor, no new hero art): add a new array to config/themes.php with values for the tokens listed in `tokens`. Add @font-face rules to public/css/fonts.css for any new self-hosted fonts and reference the family names in the font-* tokens. Run `php artisan app:apply-theme {yourname}`. This re-skins every page EXCEPT the hardcoded parts of the animated stage (see `stage.theme_specific_literals`).

2. NEW HERO LOOK: the .stage hero (public/css/hero-solarsystem.css) is purpose-built cosmic art (orbiting planets, starfield). For a non-cosmic theme, either (a) tokenize/override the literals in `stage.theme_specific_literals`, or (b) replace resources/views/partials/hero.blade.php + hero-solarsystem.css with your own hero and drop solarsystem.js. Keep hero copy bound to SiteSetting.hero (no hardcoded text).
3. To make the stage FULLY token-swappable, replace the literals in `stage.theme_specific_literals` with their `suggested_token` so a palette swap also recolors the hero chrome.
4. Keep the structure-vs-skin split: positional CSS in structure.css, all visual CSS token-driven in skin.css.

5. Preserve the test contract: skin.css must keep the strings `.hero`, `var(--hero-overlay)`, `var(--font-display)`, `var(--color-heading)`, and the CKEditor `.image-style-*` rules; config/tokens.php defaults must stay light (e.g. --nav-height: 4rem).
principles: Swap a theme by overriding tokens, not by editing markup.
Every page shares one fixed cosmos backdrop (cosmos.css) behind the content — no solar system, no parallax/3D — see the 'Shared cosmos backdrop' element.

Inner pages (About/Contact/Blog list) are token-driven — they re-skin with zero CSS edits. EXCEPTION: the single blog post body is a deliberate non-token 'paper' that mirrors the CKEditor editing surface — see the 'Blog article paper' element.
The animated Home stage is partly tokenized and partly hardcoded cosmic art — see `stage`.

UI chrome is non-selectable: nav, h1-h3, and all .stage hero text use user-select:none (h1-h3 also cursor:default); the .nav-logo img is non-selectable/non-draggable chrome; only links & buttons show the pointer. Article/body and .muted text stay selectable. Defined in skin.css and hero-solarsystem.css.

token_types.color: any CSS color; may be consumed directly or via color-mix(in srgb, var(--token) N%, transparent)
token_types.font-stack: a CSS font-family list; the first family must have @font-face entries in fonts.css
token_types.length: a CSS length; some are multiplied via calc(var(--token) * n)

color-primary.role: Primary brand / accent: links, primary button fill/border, eyebrow text, ghost-button text, scroll-cue arrow.

color-accent.role: Secondary accent / hover-highlight: link hover, nav link hover & active, button hover text.

color-bg.role: Page background; also the .stage base background and the source for the translucent nav (via color-mix). Legacy hero-cta text color.

color-bg-alt.role: Elevated surfaces: card/panel background, nav bottom border.

color-fg.role: Body / default text. Also card border tint (via color-mix) and the stage lede text.

color-muted.role: Secondary / muted text: .muted, inactive nav links, figcaption, legacy hero subhead, stage nav links (idle).

font-heading.role: Headings (h1–h3) on inner pages; conceptually the stage hero title (currently hardcoded there — see stage literals).

font-display.role: Button/CTA chrome (uppercase, letter-spaced). Formerly the text brand wordmark too, now replaced by the nav logo image.
consumed_by: h1,h2,h3 {font-family} @ skin.css (early rule; the later Solar System h1,h2,h3 rule overrides it with font-heading, so this token now only effectively paints buttons/CTAs)

space-unit.role: Spacing scale base, multiplied via calc(var(--space-unit) * n) for padding/margins/gaps.

radius.role: Corner radius for cards, content images, legacy hero CTA.

container-width.role: Max width of centered content + nav.

hero-overlay.role: Dark scrim over the LEGACY .hero starfield (not the .stage). Retained for the mystik hero + test contract.

elements.notes: Body keeps a solid color-bg (avoids a flash before the backdrop paints). The decorative starfield now lives in the SHARED COSMOS BACKDROP (see the 'Shared cosmos backdrop' element below), not on body.

elements.applies_to: all pages (fixed layer behind content; markup partials/cosmos.blade.php)
elements.notes: DECORATIVE cosmos art, the single source of truth for the starfield (the Home .stage is transparent and shows this through). NO solar system, NO mouse parallax / 3D movement — gentle ambient drift only. drift1/drift2/twinkle/pulse keyframes are defined here. For a non-cosmic theme, replace/remove cosmos.css + partials/cosmos.blade.php.

selectors: h1,h2,h3 @ skin.css line 67 (Solar System block wins)
elements.notes: An earlier rule sets headings to font-display; the later Solar System rule re-sets h1–h3 to font-heading. Net: headings=font-heading. (The former .brand wordmark that stayed on font-display is gone — replaced by the nav logo image.)

elements.applies_to: global nav, all pages (replaces the former text brand wordmark)
selectors: .nav-logo / .nav-logo img @ structure.css (centered flex item; img height 2.4rem = 3× the 0.8rem nav-link letters, 1.9rem on phone)
elements.notes: Image asset, NOT token-driven: public/img/logo-nav.png — a transparent PNG derived from res/logo.jpeg (near-white background keyed out, autocropped, rendered at 120px for retina). Links to the locale home; alt = config('app.name'). Markup: partials/nav.blade.php splits the menu 2-left · logo · 2-right. To rebrand, replace public/img/logo-nav.png (keep transparent background so it reads on the dark/translucent nav).

elements.applies_to: all pages (single partial; transparent overlay on Home via .page-home)
selectors: nav @ skin.css (translucent bg via color-mix, blur, border)
.page-home nav @ skin.css (transparent, no border on Home)
elements.notes: Layout: two equal flex link-groups (.nav-left, .nav-right) flank the centered .nav-logo. Inner pages: sticky translucent bar. Home: transparent overlay over the stage.
These ARE fully token-driven. The visually-identical buttons inside the .stage are NOT (hardcoded) — see stage literals.

selectors: .card--media @ skin.css (image card: flex column, padding:0, overflow:hidden)
elements.notes: Blog listing (resources/views/blog/index.blade.php) renders posts as a .blog-grid of cards. A post with posts.featured_image shows a square .card__media (top 2/3) above .card__body (title + excerpt, bottom 1/3); a post without one falls back to the plain text .card. The whole card is a link. Two base .card rules exist (later wins for border opacity); behavior correct, could be consolidated.

Layout/alignment for rich-text images, mirroring CKEditor's .ck-content rules so published articles match the editor: default block images center via figure.image{display:table;margin:* auto}; alignment overrides are qualified figure.image.<class> to beat the base shorthand. Class names are produced by CKEditor; do not rename. Required by SkinCssTest. NOTE: on the single-post page the real ckeditor5.css is also loaded and its higher-specificity .ck-content rules win — these skin.css rules remain the fallback/contract and cover pages that don't load ckeditor5.css.

selectors: .article-paper .ck-content h1–h6 / a (reset cosmic theme bleed) @ article.css
elements.notes: INTENTIONAL THEME EXCEPTION: the article body must render EXACTLY as authored in CKEditor, so it is deliberately NOT token-driven. The body is wrapped in .ck-content on a white sheet and the editor's own ckeditor5.css is loaded; article.css resets the cosmic skin out of .ck-content (dark text, neutral font, selectable headings). The article <h1> title sits ABOVE the sheet and stays themed (token-driven, like other page headings). Do not tokenize the paper literals — they mirror CKEditor's editing surface.

elements.applies_to: NOT used by the Solar System markup; belongs to the mystik/CSS-starfield hero. Retained for mystik + test contract.
elements.notes: The current Home hero uses `.stage` markup (see stage). These classes still render the CSS-starfield hero if a theme/markup uses them.

stage.description: The animated Solar System Home hero. Full-viewport (.stage), TRANSPARENT background — the shared cosmos backdrop (see meta.shared_backdrop / the 'Shared cosmos backdrop' element) shows through. Scoped CSS so it never leaks to inner pages. Markup: resources/views/partials/hero.blade.php (5 orbits/planets + sun + vignette + hero copy + scroll cue; the starfield/nebula/twinkle layers moved to partials/cosmos.blade.php). Behavior: public/js/solarsystem.js (parallax now moves only the solar system).

theme_specific_literals.$comment: These hardcode the Solar System icy palette / fonts instead of tokens. A palette swap via app:apply-theme will NOT recolor them. To make the stage fully swappable, replace each `value` with its `suggested_token`.

decorative_art_literals.$comment: Purpose-built cosmic art — NOT brand tokens. For a non-cosmic theme, replace or remove these wholesale rather than tokenizing. The deep-space gradient / starfield / nebula / twinkle moved to the shared backdrop (public/css/cosmos.css, .cosmos-scoped) — see the 'Shared cosmos backdrop' element. The items below are the solar system + chrome that remain in hero-solarsystem.css.

geometry_vars.--solar-scale: scales the whole solar-system cluster; set on .stage (1.18) and overridden in media queries (1.0 / 0.72 / 0.52)
geometry_vars.--px / --py: parallax offsets set by solarsystem.js on .solar-wrap
geometry_vars.--dur / --delay: per-star twinkle timing set by solarsystem.js

stage.reduced_motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) disables stage animations (orbits/planets/scroll-cue); the shared backdrop has its own reduced-motion rule in cosmos.css

fonts.used_for: font-heading (Solar System headings + stage title literal)

known_inconsistencies_for_theme_author: Stage buttons/brand/title hardcode the icy palette & fonts (see stage.theme_specific_literals) — a token-only swap recolors inner pages but not those stage elements. Tokenize them for a fully swappable theme.
skin.css has duplicate body / h1,h2,h3 / .card rules (original light-theme block + appended Solar System block; the later wins). Cosmetic; consolidate if rewriting.
Legacy .hero* classes (CSS-starfield hero) coexist with the .stage hero; only .stage is used by current Home markup.
